text.title: structured-tool-errors Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for structured-tool-errors as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Returning HTTP status code AS the error code (code: "429").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Status codes describe transport, not the agent action. Use semantic codes (UPSTREAM_RATE_LIMITED, INVALID_ARGUMENT) and let the hint enum drive the loop.

failure-mode.description: Stuffing the upstream stack trace into details.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Leaks implementation, fills context, and gives the model no actionable signal. Sanitise: keep what the agent can act on, drop the rest.

failure-mode.description: Returning the error envelope to the model on every hint and letting the model decide whether to retry.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Burns tokens, invites hallucinated retry decisions, and bypasses the runner's retry budget — the loop can spin forever.
